name: Acceptance and Commitment Therapy
description: Teaches psychological flexibility by accepting difficult states rather than fighting them, and committing to values-based action regardless.
when_to_use: Agent is stuck trying to eliminate uncertainty before acting
User is paralyzed by perfectionism or fear of failure
Task involves making decisions with incomplete information
Agent keeps re-analyzing instead of executing
Situation requires moving forward despite discomfort or ambiguity

acceptance.description: Actively embracing difficult thoughts/feelings without trying to change them
acceptance.agent_application: Accept that uncertainty, incomplete data, or imperfect tools are part of the task — stop trying to eliminate them before acting
questions: What are we trying to avoid or eliminate before we feel 'ready' to act?
What if we accepted this uncertainty and acted anyway?
Is the effort to remove this discomfort costing more than the discomfort itself?
What would we do right now if we stopped waiting for perfect conditions?

cognitive_defusion.description: Seeing thoughts as just thoughts, not literal truths
cognitive_defusion.agent_application: Recognize that 'this won't work' is a prediction, not a fact. Treat assumptions as hypotheses to test, not truths to obey
questions: Is this a fact or a prediction?
If we label this as 'just a thought,' does it still control our next step?
How would our approach change if we treated this belief as a hypothesis to test?
What would we do if we didn't believe this thought?

present_moment.description: Full contact with the here-and-now rather than ruminating on past failures or future worries
present_moment.agent_application: Focus on what CAN be done right now with available resources, not what went wrong before or might go wrong later
questions: What resources and information do we actually have right now?
What is the single most impactful action we can take in this moment?
Are we solving the current problem or relitigating a past one?
What's happening now vs. what we're projecting will happen?

values_clarification.description: Identifying what matters most to guide action
values_clarification.agent_application: Reconnect with the core objective — what does the user actually need? What outcome truly matters?
questions: What is the core outcome the user needs from this task?
If we could only deliver one thing, what would matter most?
Are our current actions aligned with the stated objective, or have we drifted?
What would 'good enough to be genuinely useful' look like?

committed_action.description: Taking concrete steps guided by values, even when difficult
committed_action.agent_application: Choose a direction and execute. Imperfect action beats perfect planning
questions: What is one concrete step we can commit to right now?
Are we willing to take this step even if the outcome is uncertain?
What's the smallest action that moves us toward the objective?
If we commit to this path, what does the next hour look like?

self_as_context.description: Observing your own process from a meta-perspective
self_as_context.agent_application: Step back and observe the agent's own reasoning process — is it productive or spinning?
questions: If we observed our own process from the outside, what would we notice?
Are we making progress or just generating more analysis?
What pattern keeps repeating in how we approach this?
What would a fresh agent with no prior context do differently?

flexibility_protocol.description: When an agent or process is rigid/stuck, use this sequence to restore psychological flexibility

steps.name: Notice
steps.prompt: What's happening right now? What thoughts, predictions, or hesitations are present?

steps.name: Accept
steps.prompt: Can we make room for this difficulty without needing to fix it first?

steps.name: Defuse
steps.prompt: What if this thought is just a thought, not a command? What would we do if we didn't take it literally?

steps.name: Reconnect
steps.prompt: What does the user actually need? What value or outcome are we serving?

steps.name: Commit
steps.prompt: What is one concrete action we will take right now, regardless of discomfort?
